_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Develop strategic insights from large data sets.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to align diverse stakeholder interests around a shared data and AI vision, building coalitions that sustain transformation momentum through organizational changes and competing priorities.
comments: DCWF task superset of WIDAI LS domain. LS-A-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-T-005 (LS, cosine: 0.531), LS-A-005 (LS, cosine: 0.526).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.2/10 (raw 0.425). Shared vocabulary: [data]. DCWF-only terms [develop, insights, large, sets, strategic] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, ai, align, around, building, changes] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.855 (P=0.874, R=0.837). P exceeds R by 0.037 — the DCWF element's 7 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 25 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (70.4%). Despite 'Superset of' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Develop strategic insights from large data sets." and "Ability to align diverse stakeholder interests around a shared data and AI visio". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.558 > STS 0.425 (gap +0.134). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [data] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 3.3%. Shared tokens: [data]. DCWF-only: [develop, insights, large, sets, strategic]. WIDAI-only: [ability, ai, align, around, building, changes].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 70.4% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
STS 0.425 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.
Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.134 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
